text: Long before the present-day politics of religious identity was seized by Islamist political activists — the so-called militant Islamists — the Reformation movement initiated by Indonesian students and civil society in 1998 has made profound change in almost every level of social and political activism in Indonesia, including terms of religion. Under the existing status quo of 1998, citizens of Indonesia had to follow, or at least appear to comply with, the politically-dictated guidelines on what constituted permissible political activity, and by extension, socially-acceptable forms of religion. The emergence of plural forms of religion at the level of belief and practical forms of worship is the result of political developments following the Reformation in Indonesia. Thus, discourses such as liberal, fundamentalist and moderate Islam have proliferated in society, and thus studying moderate Islam in Indonesia has become an increasingly important concern. It is necessary to consider the *Pesantren* (Islamic boarding schools) as the earliest institutions that planted the seed from which the movement of moderate Islam in Indonesia developed further.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written

The emergence of plural forms of religion at the level of belief and practical forms of worship is the result of political developments following the Reformation in Indonesia. Thus, discourses such as liberal, fundamentalist and moderate Islam have proliferated in society, and thus studying moderate Islam in Indonesia has become an increasingly important concern. It is necessary to consider the *Pesantren* (Islamic boarding schools) as the earliest institutions that planted the seed from which the movement of moderate Islam in Indonesia developed further.
